We do not make any profit off the cleaning rates charged to our tenants, the cleaning invoice goes to pay the vendor who was tasked with cleaning the unit. Often times it takes a while to verify information such as working with Puget Sound Energy, in which the release of a tenant's account information, despite being the Property Management company for that tenant and property does not guarantee access to billing records, as that is private information. We may get a copy of the bill, but that doesn't give us access to find out whether or not you've made a payment. We did make sure to rectify this, upon receiving all the necessary information. In regards to the change of tenancy and the duplicate leases, we are always willing to send copies of your lease agreements for you to read and review, for clarification of lease end dates. Additionally, you can always come into our office if you need help going over your lease. Again, we are sorry for any confusion and that you felt that your needs were not important to us. Going forward we hope that all your experiences with property management companies exceed your expectations.

Please know that Landmark contracts with a carpet cleaning company and they clean carpets each time a home is vacated -- at the end of a lease -- as part of our tenant changeover process. Ian,

Our team totally understands that life happens. Individuals often need or want to change their roommate situation and we work with them to make that happen whenever possible.

Anytime there is a change in tenancy -- adding or removing individuals from the original lease document -- there is a process to follow. That process takes behind-the-scenes staff time, much of which is not seen by tenants when they do things like pick up keys or sign addendums.

It is the industry standard for property management companies to charge additional fees for that service -- Landmark's $250 change of tenancy fee is included and disclosed on all of our rental agreements.
